evals.prompt: Map the customer journey for a new user signing up for and completing their first transaction on a responsive-web product. No persona document is present.
evals.expected_output: A journey map artifact written to the resolved layout path (under <parent>/journeys/<slug>.md) with frontmatter type: customer-journey. The artifact elicits or states a persona and outcome inline before dividing the journey into stages. It covers three to six coarse stages named from the customer's perspective. Each stage has four populated rows — Actions (frontstage, in the customer's words), Emotions (with valence), Pains (friction or gaps in the customer's words), and Opportunities (solution-independent). The emotional arc is summarized with the lowest-point stage identified. The map notes the platform/surface axis (responsive-web) and references MDN Responsive Design for the relevant conventions without reprinting breakpoint values. Handoff notes name the highest-opportunity stages for map-screen-flow and any implied backstage services for blueprint-service. No hex values, pixel dimensions, or framework-specific terms appear in the artifact.
assertions: Elicits or states a persona and outcome before drawing stage boundaries — the map is anchored before it is divided
Produces three to six named stages, each named from the customer's perspective (not screen names, feature names, or internal process steps)
Each stage contains all four rows: Actions, Emotions (with valence), Pains, and Opportunities
Pains are expressed in the customer's words; Opportunities are solution-independent
The emotional arc is summarized and the lowest-point (highest-opportunity) stage is identified
The surface axis (responsive-web) is noted and platform conventions are pointed to, not reprinted — no breakpoint values, pixel dimensions, or CSS syntax appear
The artifact is written to the layout-resolved path (<parent>/journeys/<slug>.md) with type: customer-journey frontmatter; the resolved path is surfaced before writing
Handoff notes name which stages and implied services feed map-screen-flow and blueprint-service respectively
No stack tokens, hex values, or values-table content appears in the output
